hero.headline: Chandigarh Online MBA in Entertainment and Media: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme with Harvard and PwC certification options

sections.tldr: Chandigarh Online MBA in Entertainment and Media covers media management, entertainment industry economics, digital content strategy, OTT platform management, talent and production management, PwC-taught modules (premium tier), zero-cost direct university EMI, and 25% Early Bird scholarship at Rs 1,65,000. NIRF #19, NAAC A+. For media, OTT, content, and entertainment industry careers.

about.body:
Entertainment and Media is a high-growth MBA specialisation suited for professionals in broadcast media, OTT platforms, digital content, film and music production, gaming, sports media, and advertising. India's entertainment and media market is one of the world's fastest-growing, driven by OTT subscription expansion, digital advertising growth, and India's rapidly scaling gaming industry.

Chandigarh University Online's Entertainment and Media MBA covers media management and economics, entertainment industry structure, digital content strategy, OTT platform management, talent and production management, and media law. The programme bridges management fundamentals with sector-specific commercial and creative production frameworks.

For Tier 2 enrollees, PwC's Entertainment and Media practice is one of the most active globally and publishes the annual Global Entertainment and Media Outlook. Co-taught modules bring live industry advisory frameworks directly into the media management curriculum.

Chandigarh University holds NIRF #19 overall and NIRF #32 in Management 2025, NAAC A+ accredited. EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all Chandigarh MBA specialisations. See the main Chandigarh Online MBA page for core subject list.

subjects.name: Strategy, Business and Globalization
subjects.desc: Media industry strategy, global entertainment market structure, IP monetisation, and platform economics.

subjects.name: Decision Science
subjects.desc: Audience analytics, content performance metrics, subscriber churn modelling, and advertising revenue forecasting.

subjects.name: Media Management
subjects.desc: Media company operations, editorial and commercial P&L, media brand management, and newsroom economics.

subjects.name: Entertainment Industry Economics
subjects.desc: Revenue models in film, music, gaming, OTT, and sports media. Rights valuation and content licensing economics.

subjects.name: Digital Content Strategy and OTT Platform Management
subjects.desc: Content calendar strategy, OTT content commissioning, streaming platform metrics (DAU, ARPU), and recommendation algorithm basics.

subjects.name: Talent and Production Management
subjects.desc: Talent contracts and management, film and series production management, co-production structures, and content delivery.

subjects.name: Project Management
subjects.desc: Film and series production project management, launch campaign management, and media product rollout.

subjects.name: AI for Managers
subjects.desc: AI in content personalisation, automated content moderation, AI-generated media, and algorithmic discovery management.

subjects.name: Media Law and Intellectual Property
subjects.desc: Copyright, performers' rights, defamation, digital media regulations, TRAI broadcasting rules, and OTT content guidelines.

subjects.name: Advertising and Brand Communication in Media
subjects.desc: Digital advertising models, programmatic buying basics, influencer marketing economics, and media planning for brands.

subjects.name: Capstone Project II
subjects.desc: Applied media project: content strategy, OTT product plan, media brand launch, or entertainment business model design.

whoHires.body:
Entertainment and Media MBA graduates from Chandigarh University Online are hired across India's media and entertainment ecosystem:

**Industries:** OTT platforms, broadcast television, film production companies, music labels, digital media, advertising agencies, gaming companies, and sports media

**Entry-level roles:** Content Strategist, Media Planner, OTT Operations Executive, Entertainment Marketing Executive, Production Coordinator

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Content Manager, OTT Product Manager, Media Agency Account Manager, Entertainment Marketing Manager, Production Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Content (OTT), Head of Entertainment (media company), General Manager (production studio), Chief Content Officer, Media Group CEO

**Top hiring organisations:** Netflix India, Amazon Prime Video, Disney+ Hotstar, Sony LIV, ZEE5, Jio Cinema, Times Internet, Network18, Zee Entertainment, Star India, T-Series, Saregama, Nazara Technologies, Dream11 media division, and digital-first media brands

skills.body:
The Entertainment and Media specialisation builds skills that media employers require at content manager and operations manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- OTT Management: subscriber acquisition economics, content cost per view analysis, DAU/MAU metrics, and catalogue management
- Media Law: copyright clearance processes, talent contract essentials, defamation basics for digital media, and TRAI compliance for broadcasters
- Entertainment Economics: rights window strategy (theatrical to OTT), music royalty structures, gaming monetisation models, and sports rights valuation
- Digital Advertising: programmatic advertising basics, video ad formats, CPM and ROAS benchmarks, and influencer contract management

**Applied skills:**
- Content Strategy: content calendar planning, genre and format selection for target demographics, localisation strategy, and content gap analysis
- Production Management: above-the-line and below-the-line budgeting, production schedule management, and post-production workflow

**Soft skills:** Pitching content proposals to commissioning editors and streaming executives, managing creative-commercial tensions in production environments, negotiating talent agreements under competitive bidding conditions, and presenting media brand strategy to advertising clients and investors.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Chandigarh Online MBA Entertainment and Media against peer programmes:

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Media Management:** NMIMS has strong media management content with Mumbai media industry connections. CU's advantage is NIRF #19 plus the PwC Entertainment and Media advisory (Tier 2) - PwC publishes the Global E&M Outlook, making its advisory insights among the most current available. Fee: CU Rs 1,65,000 Tier 1 vs NMIMS at a higher fee.

**vs Amity Online MBA Media Management:** Amity has strong media programme history through Amity School of Communication. CU's differentiation is OTT Platform Management and Digital Content Strategy subjects that reflect the current media landscape, plus NIRF #19 and PwC E&M advisory co-teaching.

**vs MICA or SIMC campus programmes:** These are top-tier specialised communication schools. Their brand is strong for traditional advertising and PR careers. CU's MBA in Entertainment and Media is the right choice for professionals already in media operations, content management, or OTT roles who need a formal management credential while continuing to work.

fees.body:
The Chandigarh University Online MBA in Entertainment and Media offers 2 program tiers based on certification bundle:

**Tier 1 (Primary) - Standard with Harvard ManageMentor Certificate**:
- Actual program fee: Rs 2,20,000
- 25% Early Bird Scholarship: Rs 1,65,000 net payable (Rs 55,000 savings)
- Payment options:
  - Annual: Rs 82,500 per year x 2 years
  - Semester-wise: Rs 41,250 per semester x 4 semesters
  - Full upfront: Rs 1,65,000 one-time
- Included certification: Harvard Business Publishing certificate

**Tier 2 (Premium Upgrade) - 3-Certificate Bundle**:
- Actual program fee: Rs 2,20,000 (same base fee)
- 18% Early Bird Scholarship: Rs 1,80,400 net payable (Rs 39,600 savings)
- Payment options:
  - Annual: Rs 90,200 per year x 2 years
  - Semester-wise: Rs 45,100 per semester x 4 semesters
  - Full upfront: Rs 1,80,400 one-time
- Included certifications (3):
  1. PwC co-created and co-taught MBA modules (unmatched in India)
  2. CAPM (Certified Associate in Project Management) certification preparation
  3. Harvard Business Publishing certificate

**Additional charges (both tiers)**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time, non-refundable)

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Chandigarh University (no external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary, a differentiator among Indian online MBAs). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options (6, 12, 18, or 24 months).

**Early Bird Scholarship terms**: Availability varies by admission cycle. The 25% Tier 1 scholarship and 18% Tier 2 scholarship are currently active but subject to timing. Reconfirm eligibility with our counsellor before enrolment.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Chandigarh University each quarter. Please reconfirm current tier options, Early Bird eligibility, and fees with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive a Chandigarh University MBA degree certificate with Entertainment and Media noted on the marksheet, issued under UGC-DEB 2020 standards. NIRF #19 and NAAC A+ credentials are recognised by OTT platforms, media companies, and advertising employers.

Tier 1 graduates receive the Harvard Business Publishing certificate. Tier 2 graduates additionally receive PwC Entertainment and Media advisory co-teaching acknowledgement and CAPM certification preparation, strengthening profiles for content management, OTT operations, and media consulting roles. DigiLocker verification is available.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Chandigarh University Online MBA Entertainment and Media alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Aakash S.
items.liked: OTT Platform Management and Entertainment Economics subjects were directly relevant to my streaming role
items.disliked: Gaming industry content needed a dedicated module given its growth
items.body: I work in content operations at an OTT platform. Digital Content Strategy and OTT Platform Management covered DAU metrics, content commissioning economics, and recommendation algorithm basics that I apply daily. PwC Entertainment and Media modules (Tier 2) brought Global E&M Outlook insights into the classroom. Harvard Business Publishing certificate added credibility when presenting to international content partners.

items.name: Priya D.
items.liked: Media Law and IP subject covered copyright and TRAI regulations accurately for digital media
items.disliked: Music industry rights management needed more royalty structure depth
items.body: I manage digital advertising at a media agency and the Advertising and Brand Communication subject gave me the programmatic and influencer economics frameworks I was missing. Media Law's digital defamation and OTT content guideline content is directly applicable to my content review work. NAAC A+ from NIRF #19 CU satisfied my agency's qualification requirement for Account Manager promotion.

items.name: Nishant K.
items.liked: Entertainment Industry Economics gave me the rights valuation framework I needed for production deals
items.disliked: More co-production and international content deal case studies would be useful
items.body: I work in film production and the Entertainment Industry Economics subject covered theatrical-to-OTT rights windows, co-production structures, and content licensing economics. Talent and Production Management covered above-the-line budgeting practically. CAPM preparation was useful for managing production milestones. Zero-cost EMI through CU made the investment manageable on a production salary.

faqs.question: What is the PwC partnership in Chandigarh Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Chandigarh University's premium tier includes full MBA modules co-created and co-taught by PwC industry experts. PwC's Entertainment and Media practice publishes the Global Entertainment and Media Outlook annually, making it among the most knowledgeable advisory teams in the sector. Co-taught modules bring this industry intelligence directly into the MBA curriculum. The PwC partnership is included in Tier 2 (premium) at Rs 1,80,400 net.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Tier 1 and Tier 2 fee options?
faqs.answer: Both tiers share the same Rs 2,20,000 base fee and identical curriculum. Tier 1 applies a 25% Early Bird Scholarship at Rs 1,65,000 net with the Harvard Business Publishing certificate. Tier 2 applies an 18% Early Bird Scholarship at Rs 1,80,400 net with three certifications: Harvard, CAPM preparation, and PwC co-taught modules. For media professionals targeting OTT strategy, content consulting, or entertainment business leadership, Tier 2 adds strong credentialing value.

faqs.question: Is zero-cost EMI really available directly through Chandigarh University?
faqs.answer: Yes. Chandigarh University offers zero-cost EMI through the university's own payment infrastructure, not through an NBFC or external marketing partner. Tenures of 6, 12, 18, and 24 months are typically available. Confirm current options with our counsellor before enrolment.

faqs.question: Does the programme cover OTT platform business models specifically?
faqs.answer: Yes. Digital Content Strategy and OTT Platform Management in Sem 3 covers OTT subscription economics, content commissioning strategy, streaming platform KPIs (DAU, ARPU, churn), and recommendation algorithm management. Entertainment Industry Economics covers subscription vs ad-supported revenue models, content cost amortisation, and rights window strategy. These subjects are directly applicable for professionals at Netflix, Amazon Prime Video, Disney+ Hotstar, Sony LIV, and other platforms operating in India.

faqs.question: Is this MBA useful for advertising and digital media agency professionals?
faqs.answer: Yes. Advertising and Brand Communication in Media in Sem 4 covers programmatic advertising, video ad formats, influencer marketing economics, and media planning frameworks. Media Management in Sem 3 covers media company P&L and brand management. Combined with the full MBA management curriculum, these subjects make the programme relevant for professionals in media agencies, advertising, and digital marketing who want a management credential with media-specific content.

chooseIf: You work in OTT, media operations, content management, or entertainment and want a NIRF #19, NAAC A+ MBA.
You want PwC Entertainment and Media advisory content (Tier 2) for content strategy or media consulting roles.
You are targeting OTT platform management, entertainment business, or media company leadership.
You prefer zero-cost EMI directly through the university.
consider: You need MICA or SIMC brand specifically for traditional advertising or PR careers.
You are targeting a creative or production career where portfolio and on-set experience outweigh management credentials.
Your budget is under Rs 1.2 lakh and a lower-fee NAAC A++ alternative is necessary.
